notes: CryptoProvider trait defines the module boundary; no formal FIPS module specification document exists
gRPC/REST API is the only interface; no direct access to crypto internals
PDP provides role-based authorization; operator role for key management vs user role for crypto operations
AES-256-GCM (FIPS 197 + SP 800-38D). Algorithm is approved; RustCrypto implementation is not CMVP-validated
Ed25519 (FIPS 186-5), ECDSA P-256 (FIPS 186-4), RSA PKCS#1v1.5 (FIPS 186-4). Algorithms approved; crates not validated
SHA-256 used on PDP wire boundary (FIPS-approved). BLAKE3 used internally for LID and encryption context hashing (NOT FIPS-approved)
BLAKE3 is used for LID derivation and encryption context hashing. While not a security function, FIPS auditors evaluate the entire module
OsRng delegates to OS CSPRNG (getrandom on Linux). OS CSPRNG is typically NIST SP 800-90A compliant but KeyRack does not validate this
Delegated to OS. No application-level entropy assessment or health testing
No Known Answer Tests (KATs) at startup. FIPS 140-3 Level 1+ requires power-up self-tests for each approved algorithm
No pair-wise consistency tests for key generation or continuous RNG tests
Keys generated via CryptoProvider::generate_key() using OsRng (software) or HSM RNG (PKCS#11)
PKCS#11 path stores keys in HSM. Software provider stores keys in process memory (not FIPS-compliant)
AES-256 keys zeroized via zeroize crate. Ed25519 best-effort overwrite. P-256 and RSA NOT zeroized in software provider
gRPC/REST over TLS. PKCS#11 keys never leave HSM. Data keys wrapped by CMK before transport
Physical security is an HSM and deployment concern, not a KeyRack software concern
Requires HSM hardware with tamper-evident seals; out of scope for software
No RustCrypto crate has undergone CMVP validation. PKCS#11 path delegates to a validated HSM module
RustCrypto crates have not been submitted to CAVP. NCC Group audit of aes-gcm exists but is not CAVP
PKCS#11 path has a clear boundary at the HSM. Software provider boundary is the process — not FIPS-defensible
